name: Assignment 1 - Recipe Domain Model - Grading Review Rubric

parts.name: Inheritance & Polymorphism

criteria.name: Abstract Class Usage

checks.name: Abstract class not properly defined
checks.description: Quantity or Ingredient is not properly defined as abstract, or abstract methods are not correctly declared.

checks.name: Concrete class does not properly extend abstract class
checks.description: ExactQuantity, FractionalQuantity, RangeQuantity, MeasuredIngredient, or VagueIngredient does not properly extend its parent class.

criteria.description: Check that abstract classes (Quantity, Ingredient) are properly used and that concrete classes correctly extend them.

criteria.name: Code Reuse

checks.name: Duplicate code should be in parent class
checks.description: Code that is duplicated across subclasses should be moved to the abstract parent class. (Can be applied up to 2 times)

criteria.description: Check for code duplication that should be refactored into the parent class.

criteria.name: Override Annotations

checks.name: Missing @Override annotation
checks.description: Overridden methods should have @Override annotation. (Can be applied up to 2 times)

criteria.description: Check for proper use of @Override annotations on overridden methods.

parts.description: Evaluate the student's use of inheritance, abstract classes, and polymorphism in the Quantity and Ingredient hierarchies. Check for proper code reuse and appropriate use of @Override annotations.

parts.name: Encapsulation

criteria.name: Field Declarations

checks.name: Field not declared as private
checks.description: Fields should be private unless there is a specific reason. (Can be applied up to 3 times)

checks.name: Field not declared as final
checks.description: Fields that never change after construction should be final. (Can be applied up to 3 times)

criteria.description: Check that all fields are properly declared as private and final.

criteria.name: Defensive Copying

checks.name: Mutable object exposed through getter
checks.description: Getters should not expose mutable internal state. Return defensive copies if necessary.

criteria.description: Check that mutable objects are not exposed through getters.

parts.description: Evaluate proper encapsulation including field visibility, immutability, and access modifiers.

parts.name: Documentation

criteria.name: Class Documentation

checks.name: Missing or uninformative class Javadoc
checks.description: Class Javadoc is missing or just says "implements X" without explaining the class's purpose. (Can be applied up to 2 times)

criteria.description: Check for presence and quality of Javadoc on public classes.

criteria.name: Method Documentation

checks.name: Missing or incomplete method Javadoc
checks.description: Method or constructor Javadoc is missing or lacks required @param, @return, or @throws tags. (Can be applied up to 2 times)

criteria.description: Check for presence and quality of Javadoc on public methods and constructors.

parts.description: Evaluate the quality and completeness of Javadoc documentation for public classes, constructors, and methods.

parts.name: Test Quality

criteria.name: Test Meaningfulness

checks.name: Excessive trivial getter tests
checks.description: Tests that just verify simple getters return what was passed to the constructor add little value. Focus on validation, calculations, and formatting. (Can be applied up to 2 times)

criteria.description: Check for excessive trivial tests that don't verify meaningful behavior.

criteria.name: Test Redundancy

checks.name: Redundant or duplicate tests
checks.description: Multiple tests verify essentially the same behavior without adding coverage. (Can be applied up to 2 times)

criteria.description: Check for redundant or duplicate tests.

parts.description: Evaluate the quality of the test suite beyond just fault detection. Check for meaningful tests that verify important behaviors rather than trivial tests.

parts.name: Code Style

criteria.name: Naming Conventions

checks.name: Naming convention violation
checks.description: Classes should be PascalCase, methods/fields camelCase, constants UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

criteria.description: Check for proper naming conventions (PascalCase for classes, camelCase for methods, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants).

criteria.name: Code Complexity

checks.name: Overly complex logic
checks.description: This logic could be simplified for better readability.

criteria.description: Check for overly complex logic that could be simplified.

criteria.name: String Handling

checks.name: String input not trimmed
checks.description: String inputs should be trimmed in constructors to remove leading/trailing whitespace.

criteria.description: Check that string inputs are properly trimmed in constructors.

criteria.name: Exception Messages

checks.name: Poor or missing exception message
checks.description: Exception messages should be informative and indicate what precondition was violated.

criteria.description: Check that exception messages are informative and helpful.

parts.description: Evaluate general code quality including naming conventions, code complexity, string handling, and exception messages.

parts.name: Reflection

criteria.name: Q1: Inheritance Design

checks.name: Complete and thoughtful response
checks.description: Response demonstrates understanding of why abstract classes are preferred over interfaces here (shared implementation, not just shared contract) and explains benefits for both hierarchies.

checks.name: Partial response
checks.description: Response addresses the question but lacks depth or only covers one hierarchy.

checks.name: Missing or inadequate response
checks.description: Response is missing, trivial, or does not address the question.

criteria.description: Question 1: Why is it useful to have MeasuredIngredient and VagueIngredient both extend a common Ingredient class, rather than an interface? What advantages does this provide for future development? Similarly, why is the Quantity hierarchy beneficial?

criteria.name: Q2: Composition vs Inheritance

checks.name: Complete and thoughtful response
checks.description: Response correctly distinguishes composition vs inheritance, explains when each is appropriate, and uses concrete examples from the assignment.

checks.name: Partial response
checks.description: Response shows some understanding but lacks concrete examples or clear distinction between the two approaches.

checks.name: Missing or inadequate response
checks.description: Response is missing, trivial, or does not address the question.

criteria.description: Question 2: MeasuredIngredient uses composition (has-a relationship) with Quantity, while ExactQuantity uses inheritance (is-a relationship) with Quantity. Explain when composition is preferred over inheritance and vice versa, using examples from this assignment.

criteria.name: Q3: Immutability

checks.name: Complete and thoughtful response
checks.description: Response addresses whether classes are immutable, explains the reasoning behind the decision, and discusses tradeoffs (e.g., thread safety, simplicity vs flexibility for recipe editing).

checks.name: Partial response
checks.description: Response mentions immutability but lacks depth on reasoning or tradeoffs.

checks.name: Missing or inadequate response
checks.description: Response is missing, trivial, or does not address the question.

criteria.description: Question 3: Did you make your classes immutable (i.e., their state cannot change after construction)? Why or why not? What are the tradeoffs of immutable objects in a domain model like recipes?

criteria.name: Q3b: Domain Assumptions and Long-Term Costs

checks.name: Complete and thoughtful response
checks.description: Response identifies specific assumptions (e.g., Western-centric units, measurement-focused cooking) and thoughtfully considers what changes would be needed for broader support.

checks.name: Partial response
checks.description: Response identifies some assumptions but lacks depth or doesn't consider implications for future changes.

checks.name: Missing or inadequate response
checks.description: Response is missing, trivial, or does not address the question.

criteria.description: (There was an ambiguirty in the spec and some students may have seen this quesiton) Question 3b: What assumptions about cooking, food, and users are embedded in this domain model? Consider what cuisines the Unit system privileges, what cooking styles might not fit neatly into "measured" vs "vague" ingredients, and what would need to change to support different culinary traditions.

criteria.name: Q4: Type Safety

checks.name: Complete and thoughtful response
checks.description: Response explains type safety benefits (compile-time checking, IDE support, no typos) and provides a concrete example of an error that enums prevent.

checks.name: Partial response
checks.description: Response mentions type safety but lacks a concrete example or clear explanation.

checks.name: Missing or inadequate response
checks.description: Response is missing, trivial, or does not address the question.

criteria.description: Question 4: How do the Unit and UnitSystem enums provide better type safety compared to using strings for units? Give a specific example of an error that enums prevent.

criteria.name: Q5: Challenges

checks.name: Complete and thoughtful response
checks.description: Response identifies a specific challenge and describes a concrete strategy used to address it.

checks.name: Partial response
checks.description: Response mentions a challenge but doesn't explain the strategy used, or is too vague.

checks.name: Missing or inadequate response
checks.description: Response is missing, trivial, or does not address the question.

criteria.description: Question 5: What was the most challenging aspect of this assignment? What strategy did you use to overcome it?

criteria.name: Q6: AI Usage

checks.name: Response provided
checks.description: Student provided a response about their AI usage (or lack thereof). Any honest answer receives full credit.

checks.name: Missing response
checks.description: No response provided for the AI usage question.

criteria.description: Question 6: Did you use AI to help you with this assignment? If so, how did you use it? (This is for course improvement purposes and does not affect the grade.)

parts.description: Evaluate the quality and completeness of the student's reflection in REFLECTION.md. Each question should be answered in 1-5 sentences with thoughtful responses that reference their code submission.

parts.name: Partial Credit for Compilation Errors

criteria.name: Unit Enum (Partial)

checks.name: Unit.java: enum declaration exists
checks.description: File contains 'public enum Unit' declaration.

checks.name: Unit.java: has required enum constants
checks.description: Enum declares at least 4 of: CUP, TABLESPOON, TEASPOON, OUNCE, POUND, GRAM, MILLILITER, LITER, PINCH, DASH, PIECE, SLICE.

checks.name: Unit.java: has abbreviation methods
checks.description: Enum declares getSingularAbbreviation() and getPluralAbbreviation() methods (method signatures present).

criteria.description: Partial credit for Unit.java when code doesn't compile. File: src/main/java/app/cookyourbooks/domain/Unit.java

criteria.name: ExactQuantity Implementation (Partial)

checks.name: ExactQuantity.java: class extends Quantity
checks.description: File contains 'class ExactQuantity extends Quantity' declaration.

checks.name: ExactQuantity.java: has amount field
checks.description: Class declares a numeric field for amount (double or similar).

checks.name: ExactQuantity.java: has toDecimal() method
checks.description: Class declares 'toDecimal()' method returning double.

checks.name: ExactQuantity.java: has toString() method
checks.description: Class declares 'toString()' method with @Override annotation.

criteria.description: Partial credit for ExactQuantity.java implementation when code doesn't compile. File: src/main/java/app/cookyourbooks/domain/ExactQuantity.java

parts.description: Award partial credit (up to 50%) for code that does not compile but contains required structural elements. This part is ONLY used when the autograder cannot run due to compilation errors. Each check verifies specific Java language features that can be detected in the source code.

description: Manual grading rubric for Assignment 1: Recipe Domain Model. This rubric covers code quality attributes assessed through manual review (subtractive, up to -30 points) and reflection grading (additive, up to +12 points). Automated grading (88 points) is handled separately by the autograder.
